title: AI Newz Snacks — 1er avril 2026
content:
**AI & Robotics Snacks** — April 1, 2026

---

## 🚕 Robotaxi Chaos Energy Is *Chef's Kiss*
Oh great, the robotaxi war is *so* civilized.

- **Waymo doubled its paid rides to 500K/week** in under a year—already crushing its 1M rides-per-year target with barely a quarter down. Zoox is quadrupling its San Francisco footprint and expanding to Austin and Miami
- **Uber playing catch-up internationally:** Partnered with Pony AI and Verne to launch Europe's first robotaxi service in Zagreb
- The utopian future where robots handle all our transportation needs is arriving whether traffic infrastructure is ready or not 🎉

**Sources:** [The Neuron](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/-around-the-horn-digest-everything-that-happened-in-ai-this-weekend-march-28-29-2026/) | [Crescendo AI News](https://www.crescendo.ai/news/latest-ai-news-and-updates)

---

## ⚡ OpenAI Is Speed-Running the Model Release Cycle

Somebody tell OpenAI that "pacing" is a thing.

- **GPT-5.4 dropped TWO DAYS after GPT-5.3**, no explanation, no warning — 1 million token context window et workflows autonomes multi-étapes
- **OpenAI projette $14 milliards de pertes pour 2026** et teste des pubs pour éventuellement être rentable avant l'IPO. Ils shippent plus vite tout en brûlant plus fort.

**Sources:** [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/category/ai/) | [Renovate QR](https://renovateqr.com/blog/ai-model-releases-2026)

---

## 🧠 Google DeepMind Quietly Eating Lunch in the Background

Plot twist: Google était déjà en avance.

- **AlphaEvolve (un agent de code basé sur Gemini) est déployé dans l'infrastructure Google depuis plus d'un an**, récupérant 0,7% des ressources de calcul mondiales de Google et accélérant un kernel Gemini core de 23%. Sans communiqué de presse.
- **Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite** : 2,5× plus rapide, 45% de meilleur débit, à $0,25 pour un million de tokens en entrée.

**Sources:** [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/31/deepmind-google-ai-race-money-hassabis-mallaby-book) | [Renovate QR](https://renovateqr.com/blog/ai-model-releases-2026)

---

## 👷 The Humanoid Gig Economy Is Lowkey Wild

Votre side hustle c'est maintenant former les robots qui vont vous remplacer.

- **Scale AI, Encord et autres recrutent des gens pour enregistrer des données pour robots humanoïdes DEPUIS CHEZ EUX.** Vous pouvez litteralement apprendre à un robot à attraper des trucs depuis votre salon.
- **Figure AI, Tesla, et Agility Robotics** en course à trois pour livrer des humanoïdes en production aux usines et entrepôts.

**Sources:** [MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04/01/1134863/humanoid-data-training-gig-economy-2026-breakthrough-technology/)

---

## 🤖 Boston Dynamics Atlas Passe en Production

Hyundai et Google DeepMind déploient le robot de science-fiction pour de vrai.

- **Atlas — le robot bipède de Boston Dynamics — n'est plus un démo.** Production déjà lancée. Soulève 50 kg, s'adapte à l'environnement, apprend de nouvelles tâches. Des flottes livraison prévues chez Hyundai et Google DeepMind dans les prochains mois.
- **LG, Sharp, NEURA Robotics** ont tous montré leurs propres humanoïdes cette semaine. C'est l'Été Humanoïde. 🌞

**Sources:** [Hyundai Newsroom](https://www.hyundai.com/worldwide/en/newsroom/detail/0000001109) | [NVIDIA Newsroom](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-releases-new-physical-ai-models-as-global-partners-unveil-next-generation-robots)

---

## 💥 Un Robot Serve Robotics Explose Une Vitre de Bus à Chicago

Un robot de livraison s'est crashé dans un abribus. Réaction du public : 😬

- **Un bot Serve Robotics a traversé la vitre d'un abribus à Chicago**, en pleine opération, au milieu de protestations croissantes sur la sécurité des robots et la congestion des trottoirs.
- "Le robot voulait juste livrer un sandwich" n'est pas une bonne défense juridique.

**Sources:** [The Neuron](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/)

title: AI Newz Snacks — 31 mars 2026
content:
**AI & Robotics Snacks** — March 31, 2026

---

## 🚗 Robotaxi Goes "Mainstream" (aka Wall Street Bets On It)

Oh great, the robo-taxi dream is finally real and we should *definitely* be excited. Waymo just hit 500K paid rides per week—that's a quarter of the way to their end-of-year goal in literally three months. Zoox is expanding coast-to-coast from San Francisco to Vegas, Austin, and Miami.

- Waymo's velocity is kind of absurd: they're doubling deployment speed, half-joking we're basically one software update away from actual autonomous chaos on I-95
- Zoox is betting the farm on "purpose-built robotaxis" (translation: purpose-built to avoid getting sued)
- When taxi drivers start unionizing *against* robots, you'll know it's real 🤖

**Sources:** [The Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/robotics-news/)

---

## 💰 Physical Intelligence Just Became a Unicorn (The Robot ChatGPT Is Worth $11B)

Physical Intelligence is raising $1B at an $11B valuation to build "ChatGPT for robots." Founders Fund and Lightspeed are leading. Yes, you read that correctly—a company that teaches robots to do warehouse tasks is worth more than most actual SaaS businesses that make revenue.

- $25B in infrastructure deals hit this week alone, because apparently we need *all* the compute for robots learning to pick things up
- OpenAI literally *killed* Sora (their video generator) to redirect compute toward robotics
- The subtext: video generation is so yesterday; robot arms are the new hype vector 🦾

**Sources:** [Crescendo AI News](https://www.crescendo.ai/news/latest-ai-news-and-updates)

---

## 🤖 Humanoid Robots Are Now Airport Greeters (And They're Multilingual)

San José's airport deployed "Jose," an IntBot humanoid robot, to help travelers in multiple languages. Because why hire people when you can have a robot that never complains about shift breaks? Also: an AI robot showed up at the Whitehouse EdTech summit with the First Lady.

- These robots aren't stealing *all* the jobs yet, but they're definitely showing up uninvited to events
- Warehouse robots with agentic AI are now autonomously handling logistics (no human intervention required, which is fine, probably)
- We're past the "sci-fi concept" phase and firmly into "deployed in actual public spaces" territory 😅

**Sources:** [The Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/robotics-news/), [RoboticsTomorrow](https://www.roboticstomorrow.com/news/2026/03/31/samsara-to-accelerate-the-future-of-physical-ai-at-humanx-2026/26342/)

---

## 🎬 OpenAI Killed Sora Because Robots > Videos (The Priorities Are Shifting)

OpenAI straight-up discontinued its AI video generator Sora and rerouted all that compute power toward robotics research. No announcement, just gone, redirected to the robot future.

- This is basically admitting: "Video generation is saturated. Robots are where the real AGI play is"
- When billion-dollar companies kill products in their prime, you know the hype cycle is pivoting hard
- Place your bets: robotics is eating everyone's GPU budgets for the next 12-18 months 🏗️

**Sources:** [The Neuron](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/)

---

## 🔄 Apple Is Giving Siri to Claude and Gemini (Antitrust Subtext Intensifies)

Apple just opened Siri to Claude and Gemini as extensions in iOS 27. Translation: Apple realized Siri can't keep up, so they're outsourcing the actual intelligence to Anthropic and Google while pretending it's "choice."

- Anthropic gets instant distribution to 2B+ Apple users (casual $100B marketing gift)
- Google gets a foothold in Apple's ecosystem (weird world we live in)
- Apple avoids building their own frontier model (smart, honestly) but also admits defeat 🍎

**Sources:** [Crescendo AI News](https://www.crescendo.ai/news/latest-ai-news-and-updates)

---

## ⚔️ Pentagon vs Anthropic: The Government Wants Skynet, Anthropic Said "No"

Over 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees publicly backed Anthropic in a lawsuit against the Pentagon. The Pentagon labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk" because they *refused* to enable mass surveillance and autonomous weapons.

- This is basically David (Anthropic) vs Goliath (US Defense Dept), except David has backing from the other tech giants' employees
- The Pentagon wants to buy AI that can surveil citizens and fire weapons autonomously; Anthropic said "absolutely not"
- Meanwhile, three Chinese AI firms allegedly coordinated distillation attacks on Anthropic's models to steal tech 🛡️

**Sources:** [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/openai-and-google-employees-rush-to-anthropics-defense-in-dod-lawsuit/), [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/03/10/google-openai-employees-back-anthropic-legal-fight-military-use-of-ai/), [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/24/anthropic-openai-china-firms-distillation-deepseek.html)

---

## 📊 The Model Avalanche Continues (12 Models In One Week—Kill Me Now)

Between March 10-16, six companies (OpenAI, Google, xAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Cursor) launched *twelve* distinct models. Four flagship models shipped in a 72-hour window. DeepSeek V4 finally arrived with 1 trillion parameters after months of hype and delays.

- Developer selection cycles are now *monthly*, not quarterly, because models are shipping faster than feature releases
- This isn't innovation anymore; it's just industrial-scale model spam
- Eventually we're going to have 10,000 models and nobody will remember which one does what 😵

**Sources:** [AI Unfiltered](https://www.arturmarkus.com/12-ai-models-launched-in-one-week-during-march-10-16-2026-openai-google-xai-anthropic-mistral-and-cursor-compress-developer-selection-cycles-to-monthly-as-frontier-model-releases-pile-up/), [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/google-openai-and-anthropic-are-all-bracing-for-deepseeks-next-big-release/), [NxCode](https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/deepseek-v4-release-specs-benchmarks-2026)

---

**That's your Snacks briefing. Same chaos, faster models, more robots. Same time next week? 🤷**

title: AI Newz Snacks — 30 mars 2026
content:
**AI & Robotics Snacks** — March 30, 2026

---

## 🤖 Google's Robot Army Grows (Shocking, We Know)

Oh great, now Google DeepMind is best friends with Agile Robots. Because one trillion-dollar megacorp controlling all the robot knowledge wasn't on anyone's bingo card.

- Google DeepMind partnered with Munich-based Agile Robots (20,000+ deployed systems worldwide) to jam Gemini Robotics foundation models into existing industrial hardware
- They're targeting "high-value industrial use cases"—because robots for mundane tasks are so 2024
- This is basically Google saying "we're going to own the physical world too, thanks"

*Translation: The robot takeover is being sponsored by a search engine company.* 📡

---

## 💸 Budget Humanoids Coming: $13K by 2035

Oh perfect, we can finally afford the robot that replaces us. Bank of America Institute just dropped the cheerful news that humanoid robot costs could plummet from $35,000 (today) to $13K–$17K by 2035.

- Material costs alone will plummet as manufacturing scales up
- This is the "physical AI" era—where AI meets sensors, hardware, and the real world
- By then, your local Amazon warehouse will have more robots than humans

*Who needs job security when you can have affordable automation?* 😅

---

## ⚡ OpenAI Drops GPT-5.4: Context Window Goes Absolutely Bonkers

GPT-5.4 arrived in early March with "1 million tokens of context"—roughly 50-100x larger than previous models. Your entire dissertation? Easy. Your company's entire codebase? Toast.

- The "Thinking" variant scored 83% on the GDPVal benchmark, basically matching human expert level
- Marketed as "most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work"
- Meanwhile your current model is screaming for more tokens like it's running on a potato

*Cool cool cool, AI got smarter while we weren't looking.* 🧠

---

## 🏃 Google's Speed Flex: Gemini Flash-Lite Joins the Arms Race

Google didn't want to be left behind, so Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite landed in March offering 2.5× faster time-to-first-token and 45% faster output than its predecessor.

- The speed game is heating up—everyone wants faster, cheaper inference
- Flash-Lite is basically Google saying "we can do responsive AI too"
- Smaller, nimbler models are becoming the flex (RIP massive parameter counts)

*It's basically model speed-dating at this point.* 🏎️

---

## 🤖 We've Officially Entered the "Autonomous Systems Era"

Plot twist: March 23-24 marked the industry's death knell for chatbots and the birth of autonomous agents. NVIDIA's GTC conference + frontier model releases from OpenAI, Google, and Alibaba collectively gasped "okay, AI is doing stuff now."

- We're past the "talk to AI" phase—now it's "AI makes decisions and takes actions"
- This is where things get spicy (and honestly, a bit dystopian)
- Enterprises are pivoting from "cool chatbot" to "autonomous workforce"

*Congrats, we collectively speedran from hype to actual deployment in like 18 months.* 🚀

---

## 💰 Mind Robotics (Rivian Spinoff) Raises $500M at $2B Valuation

Rivian spun off a robotics company, slapped a $2 billion valuation on it, and watched investors throw half a billion dollars at it. In a Series A. In *this* economy.

- Mind Robotics just hit $615M in total funding (most of which just landed)
- The bet: industrial robotics are the next frontier
- VCs are apparently still printing money and throwing it at robot companies

*Nothing says "we're totally not in a bubble" like a $500M Series A in March 2026.* 💭

---

- [Morgan Stanley warns an AI breakthrough is coming in 2026](https://fortune.com/2026/03/13/elon-musk-morgan-stanley-ai-leap-2026/)
- [Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/24/agile-robots-becomes-the-latest-robotics-company-to-partner-with-google-deepmind/)
